description_long: This object consists of two tulip-like flowers, one red and one yellow, each with a slender green stem and a pair of elongated green leaves. The flowers have cup-shaped blooms with smooth, overlapping petals that form a rounded, symmetrical appearance. The petals are thick and slightly curved, giving the flowers a robust and healthy look. The stems are straight and upright, with the leaves emerging from the lower portion and curving gently outward. The overall structure is delicate yet sturdy, typical of ornamental tulips often found in urban parks or gardens. The coloration is vibrant, with the red and yellow blooms providing a striking contrast against the green foliage.
description: Two tulip-like flowers, one red and one yellow, with smooth, overlapping petals, upright green stems, and elongated green leaves.
description_view_0: Front view showing both the red and yellow flowers side by side, with their stems and leaves clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view with the yellow flower in front and the red flower partially visible behind, leaves overlapping.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the backs of the yellow and red flowers, with stems and leaves visible from behind.
description_view_3: Side view with the red flower in front and the yellow flower partially visible behind, leaves overlapping.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration, overlapping each other to form a cup-shaped bloom with clear symmetry and layering typical of tulips.
